description: Sound: the neutral category of everything heard. Built beside 'shonui' (ear), its receiver; 'hea' (hear) is the act, 'shonuwa' the thing. Music ('meliphe') and noise ('kohura') divide it between them, and most of it belongs to neither.
sound_symbolism: Hushed 'sho' opens the air, 'nu' hums in it, 'wa' lets it reach. A quiet word for the medium of all loud ones.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'mia shonuwa hea.' (I hear a sound.) 'shonui shonuwa howela.' (The ear receives sound.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Sound is the classic meditation object after breath: always present, always changing, gone as it arrives. The bell is struck so there is something honest to attend to.

tags.aesthetic: The medium of music and speech
